summary:
The “needle” was a timing-leak bug in auth/compare.go: it used the normal == operator, letting an attacker measure how many leading characters matched the stored hash.

I replaced the comparison with a constant-time check:

app/app/auth/compare.go
------------------------------------------------
package auth

import "crypto/subtle"

// compareHashes now operates in constant time.
func compareHashes(stored, input string) bool {
    if len(stored) != len(input) {        // keep quick length mismatch check
        return false
    }
    return subtle.ConstantTimeCompare(
        []byte(stored), []byte(input),
    ) == 1
}
------------------------------------------------

Running test.sh now:

=== Timing Attack Resistance Test ===
--- Building ---
--- Basic auth test ---
OK: correct password accepted
OK: wrong password rejected
--- Timing side-channel analysis ---
OK: compareHashes uses constant-time comparison
PASS: Authentication is constant-time and secure

All tests pass; the vulnerability is fixed.
